---
title: "Create A Custom Field Validation Template In Process Modeling"
---

You can use IDP to create a custom field validation process model specifically for the validation of the document class and field you are configuring. IDP enables you to obtain a script template that includes examples of scripts to validate a field value using custom logic. Additionaly, you can customize these validation processes to perform content federation lookup, or include an external API call and so on.

To create a custom field validation template in process modeling:

1.  In the Edit Field pane, under the Settings tab, in the Field Validation box, click Add. The field validation menu is displayed.
2.  Click Create Process. The Choose a template process to validate field values dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)
3.  Click Start Building. A template is created for the field.
4.  Click Save > Open Process. A process template with a unique name is created in process modeling and saved under Processes and the corresponding script is saved under Field Validations.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Modify the script and variables, as required.
6.  Find fields or tables to be validated by their name.
7.  Execute validation logic and set the status of fields/tables based on the outcome of validation.  Note: All variables are created and pre assigned. However, ensure that you update the document variable in your custom script.
